# EMERGENCY MOTION TO RESTORE PARENTING TIME AND FOR MAKEUP PARENTING TIME

**NOW COMES** Plaintiff/Father, **ANDREW J. PIGORS**, appearing Pro Se, and respectfully moves this Honorable Court for an Emergency Order restoring his parenting time with the minor child, L.D.W. (date of birth: November 9, 2022), and for makeup parenting time to compensate for the extended and unlawful deprivation of the parent-child relationship, and in support thereof states as follows:

1. This is an emergency motion brought by a father who has been completely and unlawfully denied all parenting time with his young son for more than three hundred twenty-nine (329) consecutive days, in direct violation of Michigan statutory law, court rules, and the United States Constitution.
13. Father and Mother were previously in a relationship from which L.D.W. was born. Custody and parenting time matters have been pending before this Court under Case No. 2024-001507-DC.
### B. The Denial of Parenting Time
14. Father has been denied all contact, communication, and parenting time with L.D.W. for more than three hundred twenty-nine (329) consecutive days as of the date of this filing.
17. Father has diligently and consistently sought to exercise his parenting time throughout this period. His efforts have been met with obstruction, refusal, and interference at every turn.
20. Failing to respond to Father's communications regarding parenting time scheduling.

24. The campaign to sever Father's relationship with L.D.W. has not been conducted by Mother alone. Members of the Watson family, specifically Albert Watson, Lori Watson, and Cody Watson, have actively participated in the obstruction of Father's parenting time.
25. The Watson family members have aided Mother in physically preventing Father from exercising parenting time.
26. The Watson family members have been present during incidents of denial and have reinforced Mother's refusal to comply with Father's parenting time rights.

33. Father has complied with all court orders and requirements imposed upon him in this matter. Father has not engaged in any conduct that would justify the denial of his parenting time under Michigan law.
34. A Personal Protection Order ("PPO") was entered against Father in Case No. 2023-5907-PP.

38. As a direct and proximate result of the denial of parenting time and the litigation required to address Mother's obstruction, Father has incurred documented economic damages in excess of Ninety-Four Thousand Two Hundred Fifty Dollars ($94,250.00).
40. Michigan law provides that a parent who has been wrongfully denied parenting time may be awarded makeup parenting time, attorney fees, and other compensatory relief. MCL 722.27a(7).
### A. Michigan Law Creates a Strong Presumption in Favor of Parenting Time
46. The Michigan Child Custody Act establishes a clear and strong presumption that parenting time with both parents serves the best interests of the child. MCL 722.27a(1) provides:
> "Parenting time shall be granted in frequency, duration, and type reasonably calculated to promote a strong relationship between the child and the parent granted parenting time."

50. In *Vodvarka v. Grasher*, 259 Mich. App. 499, 512 (2003), the Court of Appeals established that before a court may revisit a custody or parenting time arrangement, the moving party must demonstrate proper cause or a change of circumstances. Here, the dramatic change of circumstances — namely, Father's complete denial of parenting time for 329+ days — more than satisfies this threshold and compels judicial intervention.

53. Father has the full capacity and disposition to provide love, affection, and guidance to L.D.W. The HealthWest psychological evaluation and Dr. DeAugustine's clearance confirm Father's psychological fitness and capacity to parent. No professional evaluation has found otherwise. **This factor favors Father**, as Father has demonstrated his capacity and willingness through professional evaluation, while Mother's conduct in obstructing the parent-child relationship calls her disposition regarding Factor (b) into question.

55. L.D.W. has been in Mother's physical custody during the 329+ day period of denied parenting time. However, an environment in which a child is deliberately alienated from a loving parent is not a "satisfactory" environment within the meaning of this factor. The Michigan Supreme Court has recognized that parental alienation undermines the stability and quality of a child's environment. *Pierron v. Pierron*, 486 Mich. 81 (2010). **This factor weighs against Mother**, whose alienation conduct has created an environment that is harmful to L.D.W.'s long-term emotional development.

56. Father seeks parenting time, not a change of primary physical custody at this time. Father offers a stable, permanent home environment in which L.D.W. can exercise parenting time. **This factor is neutral** as it pertains primarily to custody rather than parenting time.

58. Father has been professionally evaluated and cleared by Dr. DeAugustine at HealthWest. No mental or physical health condition has been identified that would impair Father's ability to parent L.D.W. No competing professional evaluation contradicts this finding. **This factor favors Father or is neutral.**

59. L.D.W. is approximately two (2) years of age and does not yet have a school record. The child's community record is limited at this age. Father is committed to supporting L.D.W.'s educational and community development as the child grows. **This factor is neutral** given the child's young age.

61. This factor is the most significant in the present case. Mother has demonstrated an absolute unwillingness to facilitate any relationship between L.D.W. and Father. For 329+ consecutive days, Mother has denied all parenting time, all communication, and all contact. This is not a case of minor scheduling conflicts or occasional difficulties — this is a complete, deliberate, and sustained campaign to eliminate Father from L.D.W.'s life.

65. Michigan courts have increasingly recognized that parental alienation constitutes a form of emotional abuse against the child. Mother's 329+ day denial of all parenting time, combined with the coordinated Watson family alienation campaign, constitutes emotional abuse of L.D.W. by depriving him of a relationship with his father during the most formative years of his development.
#### Factor (l): Any other factor considered by the court to be relevant to a particular child custody or parenting time dispute.
68. Father's full compliance with all court-imposed requirements, including successful completion of the HealthWest psychological evaluation.
71. The procedural deficiencies in the issuance of the PPO in Case No. 2023-5907-PP.
### C. The Extended Denial of Parenting Time Constitutes a Violation of Father's Constitutional Rights

75. The 329+ day denial of all parenting time in this case constitutes a *de facto* termination of Father's parental relationship with L.D.W. While Father's legal rights as a parent have not been formally terminated, the practical effect of 329+ days of zero contact is indistinguishable from termination for a child of L.D.W.'s age.
77. The constitutional violation is ongoing and continuing. Each additional day of denied parenting time compounds the constitutional injury and deepens the irreparable harm to both Father and L.D.W.
### D. Father Is Entitled to Makeup Parenting Time Under MCL 722.27a(7)

> "If a child's parent . . . has been wrongfully denied parenting time . . . the court shall do 1 or more of the following: (a) Grant additional parenting time to compensate for the parenting time wrongfully denied."

80. Father has been wrongfully denied 329+ consecutive days of parenting time. Under MCL 722.27a(7)(a), Father is entitled to makeup parenting time reasonably calculated to compensate for this extended deprivation and to restore the parent-child bond.

81. Given the extraordinary length of the denial and the young age of L.D.W., makeup parenting time should be structured in a manner that provides for a graduated reintroduction period followed by an expanded parenting time schedule that exceeds what would otherwise be ordered, in order to compensate for the lost time and to counteract the effects of parental alienation.

91. The pattern here is clear: allegations are made for the purpose of obstructing Father's parenting time and building a false narrative of danger. When investigated by neutral third parties — whether law enforcement or professional evaluators — the allegations are consistently found to be without merit.

94. **Graduated Reintroduction Schedule.** That parenting time be initially structured as a graduated reintroduction to account for the 329+ day separation, beginning with supervised or facilitated short visits (if the Court deems necessary), progressing to unsupervised daytime visits within fourteen (14) days, and progressing to overnight parenting time within thirty (30) days, consistent with established reunification protocols.

95. **Makeup Parenting Time.** That the Court grant Father makeup parenting time pursuant to MCL 722.27a(7)(a), structured as additional days, extended weekends, and extended holiday and summer parenting time to compensate for the 329+ days of denied parenting time.

100. **Economic Damages and Costs.** That the Court award Father his documented economic damages in excess of $94,250.00, including but not limited to lost wages, attorney fees, costs of compliance, and other damages directly resulting from Mother's wrongful denial of parenting time, pursuant to MCL 722.27a(7)(d).

102. **Sanctions for Frivolous Allegations.** That the Court order that any future allegations of abuse or misconduct by Mother or the Watson family be supported by specific, verified factual allegations before any restriction on Father's parenting time is imposed, and that sanctions be imposed for any future frivolous or bad-faith allegations.

103. **Guardian Ad Litem.** That the Court appoint an independent Guardian Ad Litem for L.D.W. to investigate and report on the child's best interests, including the impact of the 329+ day denial of Father's parenting time on the child's emotional and psychological development.
108. I have been denied all parenting time with my son, L.D.W., for more than three hundred twenty-nine (329) consecutive days.
109. I completed a psychological evaluation at HealthWest and received clearance from Dr. DeAugustine confirming my fitness to parent.
111. I have incurred documented economic damages in excess of $94,250.00 as a direct result of the denial of parenting time and the associated litigation.
